explanation.summary: The variable 'raw_value' is used before it is initialized.
explanation.code_evidence: The code snippet provided shows that the variable 'raw_value' is read before it is assigned a value.

fix_suggestions.title: Initialize the variable before using it
fix_suggestions.why: To prevent undefined behavior and unpredictable results.
fix_suggestions.compliance_notes: Initializing the variable to a known value will ensure that it is not used before it is set.

fix_suggestions.title: Move the usage of 'raw_value' after its initialization
fix_suggestions.why: To ensure that the variable is always initialized before it is used.
fix_suggestions.compliance_notes: Moving the usage of 'raw_value' after its initialization will prevent it from being used before it is set.

fix_suggestions.title: Add an initializer for 'raw_value'
fix_suggestions.why: To ensure that 'raw_value' is always initialized.
fix_suggestions.compliance_notes: Initializing 'raw_value' to a known value will ensure that it is not used before it is set.

risk_analysis.why: The use of 'raw_value' before it is initialized can lead to undefined behavior and unpredictable results.
potential_failures: Unpredictable behavior in the program due to the use of an uninitialized variable.

deviation_advice.recommended_decision: Implement one of the provided fix suggestions.
deviation_advice.required_justification: The use of an uninitialized variable can lead to unpredictable behavior and should be avoided.
deviation_advice.review_notes: Ensure that all variables are initialized before they are used.

explanation.summary: The variable 'x' is used before it is initialized, leading to undefined behavior.
explanation.code_evidence: The code snippet provided shows the variable 'x' being used before it is initialized.

fix_suggestions.title: Initialize the variable 'x' before using it
fix_suggestions.why: To prevent undefined behavior caused by using a variable before it is initialized.
fix_suggestions.patched_code:
5      int x = 0;       /* initialize x */
       6      int y;

fix_suggestions.title: Declare 'x' as a global variable and initialize it
fix_suggestions.why: To ensure that the variable 'x' is initialized before it is used, even if the function is called before it is defined.
fix_suggestions.compliance_notes: This fix ensures that the variable 'x' is initialized before it is used, even if the function is called before it is defined.

risk_analysis.why: Using a variable before it is initialized can lead to undefined behavior and unpredictable results.
